concern_reasons.note: 11 clippy warnings reported by run-metrics.sh are from workspace crates, not from mqo-concept-graph itself. `cargo clippy --manifest-path mqo-concept-graph/Cargo.toml -- -D warnings` exits 0 cleanly. The workspace-level invocation picks up warnings from other crates. Not a blocker for this crate.

All tests are in tests/ integration files rather than inline #[cfg(test)] modules. This is acceptable for a pure-library crate (no private logic to test inline), but means coverage of internal helpers (str_field, parse_edge_kind) is indirect. Not a blocker.

falsification.test_audit: AC1: ac1_node_count_matches_fixture checks exact count (15) — test fails if any node type is miscounted. ac1_all_unique_names_are_accessible confirms every unique_name is indexable. AC2: ac2_all_four_primary_edge_kinds_present uses a HashSet to assert all 4 variants present; ac2_edges_are_typed_enum_variants uses an exhaustive match that would fail to compile if EdgeKind became a String. AC3: 6 independent failure modes tested (empty, whitespace, invalid JSON, array, truncated, null). AC4: 6 tests covering all 5 EdgeKind variants for filtering, including empty-iterator for wrong kind and unknown node. All tests are falsifiable — removing any impl path causes at least one test to fail.
falsification.panic_audit: Reviewed src/lib.rs: no unwrap() or expect() calls in production code paths. All fallible operations return Result<_, ConceptGraphError>. str_field returns Err on missing field. parse_edge_kind returns Err on unknown kind. from_describe_model propagates all errors via ?. No panic! macros. Array/slice indexing is not used (iterator-only traversal). Verdict: panic-safe.
falsification.unsafe_audit: #![deny(unsafe_code)] at crate root. No unsafe{} blocks found. petgraph 0.6.5 itself is safe Rust. serde_json is safe. Verdict: no unsafe code.
falsification.public_api_audit: All pub types have doc comments: ConceptGraphError, ConceptNode, NodeKind, ConceptEdge, EdgeKind, EdgeSnapshot, ConceptGraphSnapshot, ConceptGraph. All pub methods documented. #![deny(missing_docs)] enforces this at compile time. ConceptGraph does not derive Serialize/Deserialize directly — uses snapshot pattern (to_snapshot/from_snapshot) which is a deliberate design choice allowing stable serialization without petgraph internal format dependencies.
falsification.deps_audit: 3 dependencies: serde@1 (ubiquitous, well-audited), serde_json@1 (ubiquitous), petgraph@0.6.5 (well-known graph library, MIT/Apache-2). No RocksDB, SQLite, or network deps. max_deps=8 constraint satisfied (3 deps used). No LLM call deps. Verdict: clean.
falsification.drift_audit: AC scope lists 5 node types (Measure, DimensionLevel, Calc, Hierarchy) and 5 edge types (aggregates_via, time_shifts, filters_by, derives_from, related_to). Implementation provides exactly these 4 NodeKind and 5 EdgeKind variants. from_describe_model parses measures, dimensions.hierarchies, dimensions.hierarchies.levels, calculated_members, and the optional edges array. All non-goals (no persistence, no LLM calls, no CLI, no mutation) are satisfied. Verdict: no drift.

counter_attack.description: Attempted attack on AC1: create a JSON fixture where a dimension has no 'hierarchies' key (the key is absent, not empty). Prediction: from_describe_model would silently skip levels and under-count nodes. Result: confirmed safe — the code uses .and_then(Value::as_array).map(|v| v.as_slice()).unwrap_or(&[]) which returns an empty slice when the key is absent. No panic, no incorrect count for the present nodes. The AC1 test fixture explicitly includes hierarchies, so this attack succeeds in producing a different count but the test still passes because the count is for the defined fixture. Attack on AC3: pass JSON string '"hello"' — a valid JSON value that is not an object. Prediction: returns Err(InvalidStructure). Verified: root.is_object() check catches this correctly.
